one_time_description: Yekau Lake Practice Bombing Range Specific Claims Settlement from Canada - $91,000,000 awarded
economic_notes: River Cree Resort and Casino operations significantly disrupted by COVID-19. Enoch Cree Nation received $11,542,723 in additional ISC COVID funding, $2,355,104 under Canada Emergency Wage Subsidy, $73,110 under Canada Emergency Commercial Rent Assistance. Nation forgave $637,150 in rent to provide tenant relief. Nation operates multiple enterprises including gas bars, cannabis shop, grocery, pharmacy, transportation, construction, farming, utilities, and real estate. Host First Nation for River Cree Resort receiving 30% of slot revenue.
notes: Large complex consolidation with 37 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 12 government business enterprises on modified equity basis. The $91M Yekau Lake specific claims settlement is a major one-time item recorded in 2021. ISC funding of $28,369,054 excludes the additional $4,411,030 ISC contribution advanced to River Cree Resort entities. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($28,369,054) + ISC River Cree Resort ($4,411,030) + CMHC ($1,121,424) + ASETS ($249,904) + Government of Alberta ($1,505,984) + Yekau Lake Settlement ($91,000,000) + FNDF ($9,919,975) + FNDF River Cree Resort ($30,319,959) - Loss from River Cree Resort investment (-$34,884,476) = ~$131.0M; osr calculated as total revenue minus identified government transfers. Long-term debt includes $60,371,909 in long-term debt plus $35,145,907 FNFA financing. Annual debt service reflects repayment of long-term debt ($1,314,605) plus FNFA repayment ($674,184) = $1,988,789. Restricted cash includes Ottawa Trust Funds ($2,540,276), Yekau Lake Trust ($55,851,183), and Settlement Trust Funds ($6,684,797). Administration expenses appear unusually high at $37.2M vs prior year $3.97M - likely includes one-time items. Prior accumulated surplus uses $88,830,454 as stated in comparative column.
